texto: They still possess virtues which might cause shame to most Christians. No hospitals are needed among them, because there are neither mendicants nor paupers as long as there are any rich people among them. Their kindness, humanity, and courtesy not only make them liberal with what they have, but cause them to possess hardly anything except in common. A whole village must be without corn before any individual can be obliged to endure privation. They divide the produce of their fisheries equally with all who come
explicacao: Orientação direta de Reuben Gold Thwaites sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “They still possess virtues which might cause shame to most Christians. No hospitals are needed am…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, communism, iroquois, útil em momentos de termino e iroquois.
